CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar May-11-2018 Off Calendar

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Mar-09-2018 Continued To May-30-18 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Feb-20-2018 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Anna Dubrovsky, Esq. 415-746-1477 for Plaintiff Elena Phillips.
Jennifer Capabianco, Esq. of Selman Breitman LLP for Defendants BAY CLUB PENINSULA, LLC, THE BAY CLUBS COMPANY, LLC.

RULING - A R G U E D. The Court shares his thoughts with the parties. Defendant argues Demurrer. (as fully reported in the reporter's notes) The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument rules as follows:

DEFENDANT BAY CLUB PENINSULA, LLC, THE BAY CLUBS COMPANY, LLC DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT.

Defendants Bay Club Peninsula, LLC and The Bay Clubs Company, LLC's demurrer to all three causes of action in the second amended complaint is overruled as to all three causes of action and their alternative motion for judgment on the pleadings is denied in its entirety. Surpassing that the demurrer and alternative motion are procedurally improper, all three causes of action in the second amended complaint are adequately alleged and that breach of contract claim does not disclose on its face that it is necessarily time-barred. Any additional information that defendants believe they need about those causes of action can be obtained through discovery. (as fully reported in the reporter's transcript)

Order is signed. (see scanned order for details)

Judge: Harold Kahn, Clerk: M. Goodman, Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR#8600, torreanom@aol.com, Reported. (D302/HK)

MOTION TO STRIKE 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Feb-20-2018 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Anna Dubrovsky, Esq. 415-746-1477 for Plaintiff Elena Phillips.
Jennifer Capabianco, Esq. of Selman Breitman LLP for Defendants BAY CLUB PENINSULA, LLC, THE BAY CLUBS COMPANY, LLC.

RULING - A R G U E D. The Court shares his thoughts with the parties. Defendant argues Demurrer. (as fully reported in the reporter's notes) The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument rules as follows:

DEFENDANT BAY CLUB PENINSULA, LLC, THE BAY CLUBS COMPANY, LLC's MOTION TO STRIKE PUNITIVE DAMAGES ALLEGATIONS 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT; REQUEST FOR JUDICIAL NOTICE.

Defendants Bay Club Peninsula, LLC and The Bay Clubs Company, LLC's motion to strike the punitive damages allegations from the second amended complaint is denied. The second amended complaint adequately alleges punitive damages liability against a corporate employer. Any additional information that defendants believe they need about the request for punitive damages can be obtained through discovery. (as fully reported in the reporter's transcript)

Order is signed. (see scanned order for details)

Judge: Harold Kahn, Clerk: M. Goodman, Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR#8600, torreanom@aol.com, Reported. (D302/HK)

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Jan-12-2018 Continued To Mar-28-18 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Dec-11-2017 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Attorney, Anna Dubrovsky of the law offices of Anna Dubrovsky (415-746-1477) appears as counsel for plaintiff/opposing party

Attorney, Jennifer J. Capabianco of the law offices of Selman Breitman LLP (415-979-0400) appears as counsel for defendant/moving party

The cause is argued and submitted by both sides as set forth in the transcript of the certified shorthand reporter.

The Court confirms the tentative ruling.

DEFENDANTS BAY CLUB PENINSULA, LLC and THE BAY CLUBS COMPANY, LLC'S DEMURRER TO FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT, as to all three causes of action in the first amended complaint filed by plaintiff Elena Phillips's is OVERRULED as to all three causes of action. The claims in this lawsuit allege invasion of an economics-based primary right, while the claims in the earlier filed case allege invasion of a physical safety-based primary right. (See Grisham v. Philip Morris U.S.A., Inc. (2007) 40 Cal.4th 623, 643 ("what is alleged are two different types of injury, one serious physical injury or injuries, the other an economic injury, giving rise to two different types of action").). After the Bay Club defendants successfully argued that they would be prejudiced if the claims alleged in this case were included in the previous case because the claims alleged in this case would significantly expand the scope of the earlier case, it is at least superficially inconsistent for the Bay Club defendants to now contend that the claims in both cases are essentially the same. It appears that there is sufficient time before the trial in the other case for the parties to complete discovery and prepare for trial on the claims in this case so that the claims in both cases could be tried together. Judicial economy strongly suggests that consolidation of the two cases may be warranted and the parties are encouraged to so stipulate. The prevailing party shall submit a [proposed] written order that tracks the Court's ruling, verbatim and that complies with CRC 3.1312.

Judge: Harold Kahn: J. Rios Merida; Reporter: Ann Delgado, CSR No. 11185, e-mail: adelgado@sftc.org (L&M302)

Notice Of Hearing On Motion To Strike Punitive Damages Allegations And Prayer From First Amended Complaint; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities; Request For Judicial Notice; Declaration Of Jennifer J. Capabianco; [Proposed] Order
Off Calendar Dec-11-2017 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Attorney, Anna Dubrovsky of the law offices of Anna Dubrovsky (415-746-1477) appears as counsel for plaintiff/opposing party

Attorney, Jennifer J. Capabianco of the law offices of Selman Breitman LLP (415-979-0400) appears as counsel for defendant/moving party

The cause is argued and submitted by both sides as set forth in the transcript of the certified shorthand reporter.

The Court confirms the tentative ruling.

DEFENDANTS BAY CLUB PENINSULA, LLC and THE BAY CLUBS COMPANY, LLC'S Motion To Strike Punitive Damages Allegations And Prayer From First Amended Complaint is GRANTED with ten days leave to amend. The first amended complaint does not allege sufficient ultimate facts to support employer liability for punitive damages as required by Civil Code 3294(b). The stipulation to strike the prayer for punitive damages in case 542559 has no effect on this complaint since that stipulation concerned the dismissal of a different cause of action - international infliction of emotion distress - not alleged in this case. The prevailing party shall submit a [proposed] written order that tracks the Court's ruling, verbatim and that complies with CRC 3.1312.

Judge: Harold Kahn: J. Rios Merida; Reporter: Ann Delgado, CSR No. 11185, e-mail: adelgado@sftc.org (L&M302)
